name: opencode-qa
tagline: QA toolkit for opencode itself: scripted CLI/server/SSE/TUI/DB checks, each script self-testing, all local.
verdict: Clean, well-scoped QA skill. All network calls target localhost/an opencode server the user runs; DB access is read-only against opencode's own local SQLite file; spawned processes use isolated XDG sandboxes so tests can't pollute the real DB. No exfiltration, no piped remote-code execution, no privilege escalation. Only gap: no explicit 'do not use for X' g

highlights.summary: No explicit negative-trigger guidance (e.g. 'not for general opencode usage help' or 'not for QA-ing other CLIs') is stated anywhere in the description or body.
